Mechanical Design

3D Modelling Creo Autodesk Inventor Drawing Mass Production 3D Print Milling Machine
Full-time Work

Project Overview

This project focused on developing new products and preparing them for mass production — from initial concept design through prototyping, cost estimation, and production validation. Products developed include electrical connectors, sockets, locking accessories, and electronic protection devices.

Key Responsibilities

Area Details
CAD Design Designed 3D models and 2D technical drawings for manufacturing using Creo and Autodesk Inventor
Tooling Feasibility Evaluated the feasibility of molds and dies for new product designs.
Cost Estimation Estimated costs for new molds and dies required for the manufacturing process
Prototyping Created prototypes using resin 3D printing and milling machines
Supplier Coordination Coordinated with suppliers to source components and compare quotations
Jigs & Fixtures Built and tested jigs and fixtures to validate assembly and manufacturing processes
Production Readiness Supported manufacturability reviews and design improvements for mass production
International Collaboration Collaborated with E-T-A Germany on new product development projects

Technologies Used

Category Tools / Methods
CAD / Design PTC Creo, Autodesk Inventor (3D modelling & 2D drawings)
Prototyping 3D printing (FDM & Resin), milling machine

Wire Peeler Simulation — New Blade Design
